Call of Bones  

Description

1: Add effect: Icebone Skeletal Form
2: Increase Mana by 8 per tick
4: Ultravision
7: Decrease Hitpoints by 11 per tick.

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 5 Skill: Alteration
Casting Time: 3 Recast Time: 6
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 0
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Fizzle Adj: 25 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Short Buff Box: No Target Type: Self
Spell Type: Beneficial Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: You feel the skin peel from your bones.
Cast on other: Soandso 's skin peels away.
Effect Fades: Your flesh returns.

Game Description

Grips your body in an unholy darkness, slowly draining your hit points while regenerating your mana.

Items with this effect

  • Soul Defiler
  • Spell: Call of Bones

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Call of Bones
ClassLevel
NEC31
Expansion:

Duration:
1 hour
  • Comments (22)
  • Sold By (3)
  • Crafted (1)
  • Screenshots
Add Image
  • Spell: Call of Bones - Research 84

Merchants:


Abysmal Sea
  • Yiem Rynawu - 8pp 1sp 5cp

East Freeport
  • Ealyson Roloius - 8pp 1sp 5cp

The Plane of Knowledge
  • Heretic Elirev - 8pp 1sp 5cp
Post Comment
confirmed

Yes he does. He's in the eastern boat. And with my CHA at 172, the price of 16.6p is correct.

Selling on Abysmal Sea
QuoteReply
# Mar 02 2004 at 1:14 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591787242164
5 posts
Hi all, this spell can be buy from Yiem Rynawu in Abysmal Sea (GoD ext) for 16.6 pp

▲ Page top
RE: Selling on Abysmal Sea
QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2004 at 7:39 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1623630127768
View User Forum
30 posts
I just went there. This spell in fact can be bought from this vendor for 16-17pp. I bought several to assure that it was not just a spell that someone sold the the vendor. Yiem Rynawu's loc in the Abysmal Sea is this: -163 -208 +98. Hope this helps.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 20 2004 at 9:18 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) umm,this is a research only spell so it was probably sold to the merchant and he probably doesnt just sell it.
RE: Selling on Abysmal Sea
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2004 at 11:39 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570662358
View User Forum
7 posts
I ran over there right after the last patch and the spell is for sale. It's no longer a research only spell.

There are a few others on the vendor that are normally npc-dropped.

#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 03 2004 at 12:37 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) yes,
skill level
QuoteReply
# Jan 01 2004 at 5:27 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591792515845
5 posts
it says trivial at 122 isnt the research formula level - 15 x 5. that would be 34-15=19x5=95.max skill is 95.that would be 27 under triv not a good success rate
▲ Page top
Regen rates
QuoteReply
# Dec 03 2003 at 2:31 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592258647715
227 posts
8 mana per tick is significantly more mana than C1 at this level. C2 can't be cast on you until level 41. As a result, during levels 34-40, this is by far your best path to mana regen. Once you ding 41, C2 will give you more mana than Call of Bnoes and no hp recourse.
▲ Page top

▲ Page top
Clarifying
QuoteReply
# May 15 2003 at 8:31 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1671409090227
View User Forum
Scholar
222 posts
So while you give yourself a mana regen, you are also casting a DoT on yourself.
Kinda like cannibalize and mage rods, but over time. (And more controlled, as you can turn it off, if you need to)

Or am I reading that wrong?

Geez, do role-players need a few swigs of dwarven spirits before puting themselves through this pain?

Edited, Thu May 15 20:51:31 2003
▲ Page top
Clarifying
QuoteReply
# Mar 21 2009 at 5:00 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592255563594
135 posts
yes, 'call of bones' regens your mana while dotting you for 16 hp per tick... but you can more than make up for this with 'vampiric curse' (level 29) which gives you back 21 hp per tick while damaging your opponent for 9 ticks... and it continues to give you 21 hp per tick through the 9 ticks even if your target dies before 9 ticks are up.

---
Jihada Thought (35 necro)
▲ Page top
RE: Clarifying
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2004 at 5:24 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591793963005
14 posts
The necro conversions r always one step above any C for that lvl anyway. When chanters r just getting Breeze, necros get a conversion that equals C1. at 34 u get a conversion that equals to C2, which is this one. Even at 64 (the last conversion u get) it is 3 times faster mana regen than C5. So yes necros r the best class as far as solo and mana regen, so what we dot ourselves, we can tap the mobs to get the hp back anyway. So its like a heal and a DD spell all in one.

Surgonan (55 Warrior)
Cuthack (33 Necro)
Grenaab (34 Beast Lord)
Arrudan (35 Chanter)
Wicrift (34 Wizzard)
Surgonon (35 Pally)
Drakons (26 Druid)

lol ive played almost every class at least once
▲ Page top
RE: Clarifying
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2005 at 4:33 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591803818376
47 posts
Actually, the parts about the low lvls are incorrect. Necros get 2/tick at lvl 8, which is the same as breeze, and chanters get it at 16. Necros do not get anything that is better than 2/tick until lvl 20, so until lvl 20, necro regen = chanter regen. Then, at 29, necro and chanter regen is the same again, because that is when chanters get c1. Only at 34 are necros permanently ahead.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 10 2003 at 8:51 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Play a necro, this spell and others like it may seem bad but it really really starts to help later when you get tons of hps and you have a pet up and you get the huge lifetaps it just keeps speeding your killing more and more and more. The final level 64 spell you can get like this for a necro is 50 mana for 63 hps every tick and you can use it while sitting on a horse since it turns you into a skeleton. Mt necro is only 29 atm but based on the mana conversions i've used they are very awesome and best of all they make you not even need KEI (since you can'y use both at the same time anyway) so in turn an even better solo player...
RE: Clarifying
QuoteReply
# Jun 20 2003 at 10:36 PM Rating: Default
Ibamn
9 posts
That is correct. You cast a dot on yourself that does 16hp per tick damage and in exchange for that you get 8 mana per tick.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 10 2003 at 12:37 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Makes you look like a skeleton, takes your life and gives it to mana.
